title: Debug and fix gallery image loading failure across all portfolio sections

requirements.text: Audit the Portfolio component's image path construction logic to identify why all 120 gallery images (residential-1.png through residential-40.png, commercial-1.png through commercial-40.png, before-after-1.png through before-after-40.png) are failing to load from the /assets/generated/ directory

quotes: Images in gallery section is not loading. Review all the source code, find every errors and debug

acceptanceCriteria: Image path construction uses correct pattern: /assets/generated/{category}-{number}.png
Category values map correctly: 'residential', 'commercial', 'before-after'
Number ranges from 1 to 40 for each category
No hardcoded or incorrect path prefixes are present

requirements.text: Verify that all 120 gallery image files physically exist in the frontend/public/assets/generated directory with exact filenames matching the expected pattern (residential-1.png through residential-40.png, commercial-1.png through commercial-40.png, before-after-1.png through before-after-40.png)

quotes: Images in gallery section is not loading. Review all the source code, find every errors and debug

acceptanceCriteria: All 40 residential images exist with correct filenames
All 40 commercial images exist with correct filenames
All 40 before-after images exist with correct filenames
Files are valid PNG format with readable image data
Files have appropriate dimensions (minimum 800x600 pixels)

requirements.text: Fix any identified errors in the Portfolio component's image rendering logic, including incorrect array mapping, off-by-one errors in number ranges, or missing error boundaries that prevent graceful fallback

quotes: Review all the source code, find every errors and debug

acceptanceCriteria: Array mapping correctly iterates through numbers 1-40 for each category
No off-by-one errors in range calculation
Error boundaries catch and log individual image failures
Component renders successfully even if some images fail

requirements.text: Enhance the Portfolio component's error logging to capture and display comprehensive debugging information for each failed image load, including the complete attempted URL, HTTP status code, network error details, file existence check results, and any CORS-related issues

quotes: Review all the source code, find every errors and debug

acceptanceCriteria: Console logs show attempted URL for each failed image
HTTP status codes are logged when available
Network errors display readable error messages
File existence checks are performed and logged
CORS headers are logged if present

requirements.text: Verify the Vite build configuration and public directory setup to ensure static assets in frontend/public/assets/generated are correctly served at runtime without being transformed, relocated, or bundled incorrectly during the build process

quotes: Review all the source code, find every errors and debug

acceptanceCriteria: Vite configuration includes correct publicDir setting
Assets in public directory are served at root level
No asset bundling plugins interfere with static file serving
Build process preserves directory structure of public/assets/generated

requirements.text: Test image loading in both development and production builds to identify any environment-specific issues, and ensure images load correctly in both contexts

quotes: Review all the source code, find every errors and debug

acceptanceCriteria: Images load successfully in development mode
Images load successfully in production build
No path resolution differences between environments
Console shows no 404 errors for image requests

constraints: Do not modify the backend/main.mo file as image loading is a frontend-only issue
Preserve all existing Portfolio component functionality including category filtering and project descriptions
Maintain the existing image naming convention pattern: {category}-{number}.png
Do not change the immutable paths listed in frontend.immutablePaths
nonGoals: Adding new gallery categories beyond residential, commercial, and before-after
Changing the total number of images from 120 (40 per category)
Implementing lazy loading or progressive image loading features
Adding image optimization or compression features
Creating a backend image storage system
